name: Right Scapula
description: Flat triangular bone of the shoulder girdle. Its glenoid cavity receives the head of the humerus; the acromion and coracoid process are prominent landmarks.
articulations: Head of the humerus (at the glenoid cavity) — glenohumeral (shoulder) joint
Acromial end of the clavicle (at the acromion) — acromioclavicular joint
muscleAttachments: Supraspinatus and infraspinatus — supraspinous and infraspinous fossae
Trapezius, rhomboids, serratus anterior — borders and spine
clinical: Scapular fractures are uncommon and, due to the protected location, usually signal severe chest trauma.
Winged scapula results from serratus anterior paralysis (long thoracic nerve injury).
